Innovation in medtech doesn't happen in boardrooms, it happens in the grind, where [#engineers](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fengineers&trk=public_post-text), investors & visionaries collide. That's the pulse behind iRiva Medical, Inc., a Minneapolis-based spinout from accelerator NuXcel that's turning [#neuromodulation](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fneuromodulation&trk=public_post-text) into a weapon against [#chronicdisease](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fchronicdisease&trk=public_post-text). iRiva is the 4th company born from NuXcel's stable, after ShiraTronics, Avivomed & iVEAcare, and like its predecessors, it's built on decades of hard-earned Class III [#deviceexpertise](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdeviceexpertise&trk=public_post-text), from concept to clinic to commercial scale. On Nov 11, 2025, iRiva announced its initial financing round (closed Nov 4). Amount undisclosed, but the syndicate speaks volumes. [F-Prime](https://www.linkedin.com/company/f-prime-capital-partners?trk=public_post-text) Capital led, joined by [Hatteras Venture Partners](https://www.linkedin.com/company/hatteras-venture-partners?trk=public_post-text), [Norwest](https://www.linkedin.com/company/norwest-venture-partners?trk=public_post-text) Venture Partners &[Treo Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/treoventures?trk=public_post-text). That's not just capital, it's conviction. These are firms that back platforms built to redefine standard-of-care, not chase trends. Their bet: iRiva's tech can sense, adapt & treat [#chronicconditions](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fchronicconditions&trk=public_post-text) with precision no one's pulled off yet. President & CEO Steve Masson isn't some newcomer chasing a headline. With 40+ yrs in med devices, 35 in implantables, he's a living archive of innovation. From building cardiac pacemakers at Cordis to leading defibrillator tech at Ventritex (acquired by [St. Jude Medical](https://www.linkedin.com/company/st--jude-medical?trk=public_post-text) for $505M), Masson's fingerprints are all over medtech's evolution. He co-founded InnerPulse & NeuroTronik, later running Avivomed as CEO. You don't hire Steve Masson to experiment, you hire him to execute. Co-founder & Chairman [Mudit K. Jain, PhD](https://www.linkedin.com/in/muditkjain?trk=public_post-text), PhD, brings equal firepower. As Founding GP at Treo Ventures & CEO of NuXcel, Jain has co-founded 4 neuromodulation cos & helped raise $175M+ across early rounds. His board résumé reads like a Nasdaq ticker parade, CVRx, Inspire Medical Systems, iRhythm Technologies, and his exits tell the story: [Aptus.AI](https://it.linkedin.com/company/aptus-ai?trk=public_post-text) Endosystems (Medtronic), [CardioMEMS, Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cardiomems-inc.?trk=public_post-text) (St. Jude), [Impres](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/impres-inc?trk=public_post-text) Medical (J&J). That's not luck, that's pattern recognition. Backing them is Co-founder & Director [Lynn Elliott](https://www.linkedin.com/in/lynn-elliott-0769955?trk=public_post-text), a 30-yr veteran of active implantables with stints at Guidant, Boston Scientific & Spinal Modulation (sold to St. Jude/Abbott for $500M+). Elliott's known for turning R&D into ROI, bridging the gap between the lab & the OR. The global neuromodulation market hit $5.8B in 2024 & is projected to hit $10.39B by 2030 (CAGR 8.5%). The giants, [Medtronic](https://www.linkedin.com/company/medtronic?trk=public_post-text), [Abbott](https://www.linkedin.com/company/abbott-?trk=public_post-text), Boston Scientific, own ~65% of it, but innovation always leaks from the edges. iRiva's lean model, born inside NuXcel, is built for speed & precision, translating clinical insight into scalable, smart [#implantsystems](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fimplantsystems&trk=public_post-text) designed to treat real patients, not just impress investors. [Cala Health](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cala-health-inc?trk=public_post-text) announced the close of an oversubscribed $50 million growth round led by Vertex Growth Fund and [Nexus NeuroTech Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/nexus-neurotech-ventures?trk=public_post-text), with participation from existing investors including Johnson & Johnson Innovation – JJDC, OSF Ventures, [Lux Capital](https://www.linkedin.com/company/lux-capital?trk=public_post-text), and [GV (Google Ventures)](https://www.linkedin.com/company/googleventures?trk=public_post-text). San Francisco–based [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text), a longevity diagnostics company founded by Dr. Irina Conboy and led by CEO [Alina Rui Su](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alinasu?trk=public_post-text), has raised $11 million in seed funding, bringing its total to $15 million. The round was led by [Accel](https://www.linkedin.com/company/accel-vc?trk=public_post-text), with participation from [Samsung Next](https://www.linkedin.com/company/samsung-next?trk=public_post-text), Zone2, Steve Aoki’s Aoki Labs, Giannis Antetokounmpo’s Build Your Legacy (BYL) Ventures, and [Simu Liu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simu-liu-47946a2b1?trk=public_post-text)’s [Markham Valley Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/markhamvalley?trk=public_post-text). Generation Lab’s flagship SystemAge™ platform measures biological aging across 19 physiological systems, helping clinicians detect early aging signals and guide preventative care. Portfolio News

Oct 22, 2025

# Creating the Standard for Longevity: Our Seed Investment in Generation Lab

written by

Kerry Wang

![Creating the Standard for Longevity: Our Seed Investment in Generation Lab](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6661f5618fe795fa8894d04f/68f93bc5edba3321dc1e204f_Generation%20Lab%20Blog.avif)

For decades, aging has been treated as an inevitability. We’ve had plenty of wellness trends –  from cold plunges to juice cleanses – but little in the way of precise, science-backed tools to slow the biological clock, until now. The longevity market is growing fast, fueled by post-COVID health empowerment, high-profile biohackers like [Bryan Johnson](https://www.accel.com/relationships/braintree) (also an [Accel](https://www.accel.com/) founder), and the arrival of therapies that shift aging from “unavoidable decline” to “treatable condition.”

But to treat a problem, you need to measure it. Enter [Generation Lab](https://www.generationlab.com/), a company co-founded by renowned longevity researcher Dr. Irina Conboy, that is defining how we measure and eventually treat human aging.

Generation Lab’s SystemAge test uses a patented assay to analyze DNA methylation noise from a small at-home blood sample, producing an organ-by-organ biological age report. In addition, every individual test comes with personalized, science-backed recommendations including everything from therapeutic plasma exchange to supplements and workouts. Today, it’s the most accurate test on the market with 99.9% diagnostic accuracy, and trusted enough that over 275 clinics are already using it to recommend therapies and prove efficacy to patients. For me, the test surfaced a pre-existing condition that was invisible to a standard blood panel. I’m now proactively taking treatments, as well as giving the test to my entire family.

This is not another wellness app or one-size-fits-all health report. It’s a pick-and-shovel tool for the entire longevity ecosystem: clinicians, therapy providers, pharma R&D teams, and consumers at home. And it’s just the beginning. The team’s ambition is to make biological age testing as routine as a cholesterol panel and to leverage their science to develop anti-aging therapies themselves.

The founding trio behind Generation Lab is uniquely qualified to make this happen. CEO Alina Su is a Harvard PhD dropout and longevity lab researcher who combines scientific depth with relentless drive. CSO Irina Conboy is a tenured Berkeley professor and pioneer of the rejuvenating effects of parabiosis, bringing 20 years of groundbreaking aging research. COO Michael Suswal is a repeat unicorn hard-tech founder with a personal mission to extend healthy human lifespan. Their combination of cutting-edge science, operational excellence, and founder grit is rare.

Today, I’m thrilled to announce that [Accel](https://www.accel.com/) is leading Generation Lab’s seed financing. We couldn’t be more excited to partner with Alina, Michael, Irina, and the team on their charge to become the standard for longevity. If you want to extend your own healthspan, [use their test](https://www.generationlab.com/the-systemage-test?via=debda1) and [check out their open roles](https://www.generationlab.com/careers).

![Creating the Standard for Longevity: Our Seed Investment in Generation Lab](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6661f5618fe795fa8894d04f/68f93bc5edba3321dc1e204f_Generation%20Lab%20Blog.avif)

results.content: global and personalized medicine. [Generation Lab Raises $11M Seed Round as SystemAge™ Becomes World's Fastest-Growing Longevity Diagnostic](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/generation-lab-raises-11m-seed-round-as-systemage-becomes-worlds-fastest-growing-longevity-diagnostic-302592643.html)

Among the top 1% of startup raises, the round will accelerate adoption of the SystemAge breakthrough aging diagnostic platform across 275+ clinics and tens of thousands of people worldwide SAN FRANCISCO, Oct. 23, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Generation Lab, the company delivering breakthroughs in longevity science, today announced the completion of an $11 million seed funding round, bringing total funding to $15 million and placing the company in the top 1% of all startup seed raises. Led by Accel, the round also includes notable investors Samsung Next, Zone2, EDM legend Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, NBA star Giannis Antetokounmpo's Build Your Legacy (BYL) Ventures, and actor Simu Liu's Markham Valley Ventures. Less than ten months post-launch, Generation Lab has achieved remarkable commercial traction with its SystemAge™ quantification of biological age—health of 19 organ systems/functions from intact primary blood results—that is based on discovery of the physiologic non-linear curve of DNA methylation aging. SystemAge greatly improves on alternatives that make predictions from man-made graphs after transforming data in a biologically arbitrary way. In less than a year of operation, the company has signed over 275 clinics worldwide, amassed more than 300 million human-aging data points, and achieved 99.9% diagnostic accuracy, providing for the first time a clinically relevant capacity for meaningful, well-controlled healthspan optimization—the next frontier in global and personalized medicine. Market Leadership Through Clinical Adoption SystemAge™ has rapidly gained adoption across diverse healthcare settings, from leading academic medical centers to boutique wellness practices. The platform is trusted by renowned longevity physicians, professional athletes, and celebrities, with early advocates including leading longevity experts Professors David Sinclair and George Church of Harvard Medical School, Dr. Adeel Khan, award-winning author Dr. Michael Roizen, and Aubrey de Grey. "We're witnessing a fundamental shift from reactive to proactive healthcare—led by consumers," said Alina Su, CEO and Co-founder of Generation Lab. "Our explosive growth, from zero to 275+ clinic partners in under a year, demonstrates that both clinicians and consumers are hungry for actionable aging insights. This funding allows us to accelerate our mission of making longevity diagnostics the standard of care globally. We're not just measuring aging; we're creating the roadmap to reverse it." "Generation Lab is shaping the future of the longevity industry," said Kerry Wang, Partner at Accel. "Alina, Michael, and Irina are building with conviction and credibility in a space that demands both. Their SystemAge™ platform combines solid science with strong commercial progress, creating a new category in data-driven healthcare. This is only the beginning." Revolutionary Science Driving Results SystemAge™ measures the progression of biological aging across 19 distinct physiological systems (cardiovascular, reproductive, immune, fibrotic, metabolic, regenerative, etc.), with comprehensive mechanistic insights that enable effective personalized intervention strategies. The platform's breakthrough dataset has already established striking causality between SystemAge-found biological dysregulation—noise and disease-propensity—later confirmed in clinical tests that would not have been otherwise prescribed to pre-symptomatic individuals. In contrast to subjective so-called biological clocks that make correlative predictions from biologically arbitrary numbers with decades-large errors, SystemAge objectively and accurately quantifies changes critical for health from intact primary data. Interestingly, 70% of tested individuals were found to be biologically older than their chronological age, and sadly, company founders age 1.5 times faster than investors. "What sets SystemAge™ apart is our systems-based approach to aging measurement using what we're calling 'biological noise,'" explained Dr. Irina Conboy, Co-founder of Generation Lab and renowned aging researcher at UC Berkeley. "While other platforms offer disease biomarkers, we've developed the first preventive health test detecting signals before symptoms appear. This is the most comprehensive aging diagnostic, analyzing how multiple organ systems age in concert. Our proprietary algorithms, trained on hundreds of millions of data points, don't just tell you how old you are—they reveal which specific systems are aging fastest and provide precise interventions to reverse the process." Proven Clinical Impact Real-world clinical data demonstrates SystemAge™'s ability to guide effective interventions. Clinical studies of SystemAge before versus after MUSE Stemcell treatment have shown 13.6 years of brain age reversal and 4.9 years of reproductive system age reversal. Anti-inflammatory diets and targeted fasting protocols can measurably slow organ aging, while optimized exercise regimens can reverse heart and immune system biological age by multiple years within six months. The platform is detecting aging signals before symptoms appear, enabling truly preventative care pathways that could transform healthcare economics and patient outcomes. About Generation Lab Founded by renowned aging researcher Dr. Irina Conboy, Generation Lab is pioneering the future of longevity diagnostics through its flagship SystemAge™ platform. The company's mission is to create the Ageless Generation by providing the most comprehensive, accurate, and actionable aging diagnostics available. [Generation Lab Emerges from Stealth with Funding Round and Mission to Extend the Human Healthspan – Waitlist for Its Clinically Driven At-Home Aging Test + Personalized Aging Intervention Opens Today](https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/ReleaseNg/6044583)

With roots at University of California at Berkeley, startup aims to reduce healthcare costs and help people live longer, healthier livesSAN FRANCISCO, Jan. 30, 2024 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Generation Lab, which is extending the human healthspan with the first test based on biomedical evidence that measures an individual’s biological age progression, officially launched today out of stealth. The company is announcing its pre-seed funding and the opening of its test kit waitlist. Co-founded by Irina Conboy, a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time. According to the CDC, six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do. Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise - a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc. Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023. “Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early - even when a person is pre-symptomatic - which leads to better outcomes.” George Church, Ph. D., the Robert Winthrop Professor of Genetics at Harvard Medical School, is a re-known geneticist who is serving as an advisor to Generation Lab. “Irina and her team are taking cutting edge research straight out of the lab and applying it to a pressing issue: identifying disease much earlier than ever before possible using genetic markers,” said Dr. Church. “Seeing this unfold, and having a front-row seat to its impact, is going to be a pivotal moment in epigenetics – it’s incredibly exciting.” Here’s how Generation Lab works: Obtain a test kit from a physician, clinic, or from Generation Lab directly.Open the kit and scan the QR code, selecting your level of membership (entry-level subscriptions start at about $400 per year for three tests).Follow the directions for a cheek swab and mail the sample back to Generation Lab in a pre-addressed envelope or return it to the clinician who provided the test.Within a few weeks, Generation Lab will provide a personalized report highlighting the status of your specific molecular aging markers, and offering a virtual call with a physician to review clinical and lifestyle interventions you might consider to slow the aging process or improve health.After subsequent Generation Lab tests, your clinician can review how effective each intervention was and its impact on your health and rate of aging. Generation Lab’s other two co-founders are Alina Rui Su and Michael Suswal. Su, who studied longevity at Harvard Medical School and Berkeley, was previously founder and CEO of NovaXS. She serves as Generation Lab’s CEO, setting the vision and leading the company’s development. Suswal, who was previously co-founder and COO of tech startup Standard AI (where he remains on the Board), serves as COO, leading Generation Lab’s operations and commercialization efforts. “As rejuvenation research expands, we need to focus on identifying the most effective measurement for aging. My passion in healthcare led me to focus on Aging & Regeneration since I was a researcher in Irina’s lab at Berkeley,” said Su. “Generation Lab’s technology has the potential to impact an 8-billion-person global population. We will be a one-stop shop for testing plus clinician recommendations for interventions with the ability to ship personalized products to your doorstep. I’m proud that we’re making it easy for people to take a more proactive role in their healthcare and better understand their risk of aging factors.” Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit generationlab.co. About Generation Lab Generation Lab aims to extend the human healthspan with the first test based on clinically relevant biological evidence that measures individuals’ biological age progression and risk of disease so everyone can live longer, healthier lives. Co-founded by a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time. Join the waitlist at generationlab.co. Here’s how Generation Lab works:

* Obtain a test kit from a physician, clinic, or from Generation Lab directly.
* Open the kit and scan the QR code, selecting your level of membership (entry-level subscriptions start at about $400 per year for three tests).
* Follow the directions for a cheek swab and mail the sample back to Generation Lab in a pre-addressed envelope or return it to the clinician who provided the test.
* Within a few weeks, Generation Lab will provide a personalized report highlighting the status of your specific molecular aging markers, and offering a virtual call with a physician to review clinical and lifestyle interventions you might consider to slow the aging process or improve health.
* After subsequent Generation Lab tests, your clinician can review how effective each intervention was and its impact on your health and rate of aging.

Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do.

Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc.  Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early – even when a person is pre-symptomatic – which leads to better outcomes.”

Co-founded by [Irina Conboy](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATUU2LHKSbPg854wTzRTSv0e9PJ582xD6DNSESk2gn6QZaexS60B0sE2Zi9kyeK2jSBQ-3D-3DBHg5_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioJD040nHobNbpWz3V5eSPxVO0LZnsm0kHydeleYuGZWPAqLGD-2FWNnuMi78Y3hx-2FJJs7syIuUdGHZgTzLq0IeYHUkTq3kHAojLc41S7LnQF6xFlpjM1CvwfSNSR66Uv5gq7prLU1SDZd7kqOHJAnDKs-3D), a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time.

According to the [CDC](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATURHuYuDkY-2F8kFuqzju5-2BdQf-2BplA0M-2BpvnD5M2MUQMvg8jjSButV23RpULQLJ66ySbw-3D-3DedM0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioyR5HW0MF-2F2k3wNtm38leZlFS8yrqfiu3ULrrxw8OXqO-2B-2FZRj7Zcq7E8zOrI9yqVhDXhwF7MbNUczj9zCLnhvMv0yj1ULZBvLvu2NQFYWgL-2B36K94LDfNISp4AuDz5ggr5YCIh28H2vxG1TnuAhq6M-3D), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Catching these conditions early and preventing their progression can save millions of lives and billions of dollars. That’s what Generation Lab plans to do.

Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc.  Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking research led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in Aging in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early – even when a person is pre-symptomatic – which leads to better outcomes.”

Here’s how Generation Lab works:

* Obtain a test kit from a physician, clinic, or from Generation Lab directly.
* Open the kit and scan the QR code, selecting your level of membership (entry-level subscriptions start at about $400 per year for three tests).
* Follow the directions for a cheek swab and mail the sample back to Generation Lab in a pre-addressed envelope or return it to the clinician who provided the test.
* Within a few weeks, Generation Lab will provide a personalized report highlighting the status of your specific molecular aging markers, and offering a virtual call with a physician to review clinical and lifestyle interventions you might consider to slow the aging process or improve health.
* After subsequent Generation Lab tests, your clinician can review how effective each intervention was and its impact on your health and rate of aging.

“As rejuvenation research expands, we need to focus on identifying the most effective measurement for aging. According to the [CDC](https://u7061146.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=4tNED-2FM8iDZJQyQ53jATURHuYuDkY-2F8kFuqzju5-2BdQf-2BplA0M-2BpvnD5M2MUQMvg8jjSButV23RpULQLJ66ySbw-3D-3DedM0_TIUcMwzJEyy2Sf9Tw0-2BD1ji7lvoeVo1QHbCfgo5PwaA0QxzO1GSTLMu09Mmf36or1WRiNw1uy3cyqQamaEM-2BawiUZevxu8GNBA-2Fbb6dyRppH2RgJvthLEAPbbppMmnlMEBqhRGhU4KQmD9q0OU3QBtqb07U1ebc0SwO1MOYd0XuDZ1UL7zQPR2MOEMUdzyStYiCuqYTDrGAk4gC9B4JoioyR5HW0MF-2F2k3wNtm38leZlFS8yrqfiu3ULrrxw8OXqO-2B-2FZRj7Zcq7E8zOrI9yqVhDXhwF7MbNUczj9zCLnhvMv0yj1ULZBvLvu2NQFYWgL-2B36K94LDfNISp4AuDz5ggr5YCIh28H2vxG1TnuAhq6M-3D), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. Unlike biological clocks, which are based on linear models that predict aging and disease by comparing a few people to each other, Generation Lab quantifies the actual biological age of each person through precise measurement of molecular biological noise – a key barometer of aging. # Generation Lab Emerges from Stealth with Funding Round and Mission to Extend the Human Healthspan – Waitlist for Its Clinically Driven At-Home Aging Test + Personalized Aging Intervention Opens Today

## With roots at University of California at Berkeley, startup aims to reduce healthcare costs and help people live longer, healthier lives

 | Source:  [Generation Lab](/en/search/organization/Generation%2520Lab)     Generation Lab

---

SAN FRANCISCO, Jan. 30, 2024 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- [Generation Lab](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=Gjhkmu4F5V6D8AfQRdGvpOnEq0rHjPD562_fuJc6oqSPiTdWatJqLNJyNyhJaFni00FDKzFcOke3lX4slvCbMIM3L0xT0I90kWpqk7C7X7A=), which is extending the human healthspan with the first test based on biomedical evidence that measures an individual’s biological age progression, officially launched today out of stealth. The company is announcing its pre-seed funding and the opening of its [test kit waitlist](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=ejQJBzzBU_sQSihZd9DgUAH3dH2HzBzm-Ngw-vao9JsFbRL6zvicdHWjwGVfg7-csWfe79kMo4SC8QAEzFsasg==).

Co-founded by [Irina Conboy](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=rDtfqhRgq9ZCsDcL0bHo5WAUlnthEkYwC7eVTTfAfkh6gCEVgViDF8OyqapMIjT0roPzSYJ0e9N0gN4eX5MEWmsh3dzKg_Nl8bFKWCn33ln8eibO7OLwz_ro1YlekaoL), a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time.

According to the [CDC](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=VJDdv00QmEkNK2wuDqAFHXt68NgIxWvJBlXBZcyc3MQUyaQiG-wfw_j2LcOUhB8Ckst-Bkb6IhOEdymyw9W8MtH9OlKsweKiucvhSorL2Z8=), six in ten adults in the US have at least one chronic disease and four in ten adults have two or more. Many of these are correlated with aging. These diseases are a major cause of morbidity and mortality and place a significant burden on our healthcare system. Moreover, the biological age is quantified by Generation Lab for each function of the body: inflammation, regeneration, homeostasis, reproduction, etc. Generation Lab’s solution is based on peer-reviewed science – including groundbreaking [research](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=kObsWtnBgPRpTkxemnKPEAz6XXgnSDeVzhCwqoBt6x4M5urxy-5OVhIFiWyNRtW8ZXnKf4ecRnt75O8v-6Rjrh5RWvcFUXuFO_sxb_o4WIM=) led by Dr. Irina Conboy, UC Berkeley, and published as a cover story in *Aging* in Sept 2023.

“Certain molecular disbalance causes degenerative diseases that flare up with aging,” explained Dr. Conboy, co-founder of Generation Lab, and Professor at University of California at Berkeley. “Generation Lab’s tests enable us to identify when a person has a proclivity towards certain conditions and suggest approaches that can help to attenuate, delay or even eliminate those risks. This paves the way to novel anti-aging medicine for identifying and treating diseases early - even when a person is pre-symptomatic - which leads to better outcomes.”

[George Church, Ph. D](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=lXfVozd3iKkcFQPRVlff664mDzSE1nsflqDW2hsVQb-0bxk-FIu2nPJqqw3_8HOTjW4_KfakUbZc8erJtYN3sUyUfaGAcpqWsSQkY8EOjr9CUGXzpn8uxymRuDvud7m5-P-iwYQ3raAzETLe0-X6ww==)., the Robert Winthrop Professor of Genetics at Harvard Medical School, is a re-known geneticist who is serving as an advisor to Generation Lab. “Irina and her team are taking cutting edge research straight out of the lab and applying it to a pressing issue: identifying disease much earlier than ever before possible using genetic markers,” said Dr. Church. “Seeing this unfold, and having a front-row seat to its impact, is going to be a pivotal moment in epigenetics – it’s incredibly exciting.”

Here’s how Generation Lab works:

* Obtain a test kit from a physician, clinic, or from Generation Lab directly.
* Open the kit and scan the QR code, selecting your level of membership (entry-level subscriptions start at about $400 per year for three tests).
* Follow the directions for a cheek swab and mail the sample back to Generation Lab in a pre-addressed envelope or return it to the clinician who provided the test.
* Within a few weeks, Generation Lab will provide a personalized report highlighting the status of your specific molecular aging markers, and offering a virtual call with a physician to review clinical and lifestyle interventions you might consider to slow the aging process or improve health.
* After subsequent Generation Lab tests, your clinician can review how effective each intervention was and its impact on your health and rate of aging.

She serves as Generation Lab’s CEO, setting the vision and leading the company’s development. Suswal, who was previously co-founder and COO of tech startup Standard AI (where he remains on the Board), serves as COO, leading Generation Lab’s operations and commercialization efforts.

“As rejuvenation research expands, we need to focus on identifying the most effective measurement for aging. My passion in healthcare led me to focus on Aging & Regeneration since I was a researcher in Irina’s lab at Berkeley,” said Su. “Generation Lab’s technology has the potential to impact an 8-billion-person global population. We will be a one-stop shop for testing plus clinician recommendations for interventions with the ability to ship personalized products to your doorstep. I’m proud that we’re making it easy for people to take a more proactive role in their healthcare and better understand their risk of aging factors.”

Generation Labs has raised a pre-seed round from Transpose Platform and Sequoia China Founding Partner. The company expects its aging test kits to be available in Q1 2024. To join the waitlist, visit generationlab.co.

**About Generation Lab**

[Generation Lab](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=Gjhkmu4F5V6D8AfQRdGvpAZUBgYXpRh7aRp6DDUeQShc9g-mHdgVKv_2a-Hem9HQoGjMBJLrXQDa3PaQxMpTq_YcN7meqk_df23QUHMh5AIKUJU2IU4bJZXAU-zX4D0n92rcZriSsWGT2dX7jDJFOJUFJk1_IPw_NH6gAGCJCNc=) aims to extend the human healthspan with the first test based on clinically relevant biological evidence that measures individuals’ biological age progression and risk of disease so everyone can live longer, healthier lives. Co-founded by a preeminent aging and longevity researcher from UC Berkeley, Generation Lab combines a simple cheek-swab test with proprietary techniques to definitively measure molecular disbalance that indicates aging and risk of disease. The company works with clinicians who can recommend personalized interventions, and Generation Lab can measure the effectiveness of these over time. Join the waitlist at [generationlab.co](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=6F01H02J0DmvtVvU2dq281DLFnzuTlYKlu0T9ZpYpRRt5iKvi_JXwtb0LB1ud71hJHdrBtPu1bsJmXmnSMBaM_Ek3LBFc__uBRyCHod88wWC87NferSXBT8-VCSDZrYTUPevL_8Jgg9TXbaKjWyWyw==).

results.content: professor whose #research on plasma dilution flipped #agingbiology on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of Standard AI and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact. Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your #biologicalage, it measures it. With over 460 #epigenetic markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your #brain, #heart, #liver, or #immunesystem really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year #brainage reversal using MUSE #Stemcell treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data. This seed round, featuring Samsung Next, Zone2, Steve Aoki's Aoki Labs, [...] Adaptyx Biosciences just raised a $14M seed round, and it’s not another wearable chasing vanity metrics. This Menlo Park crew is decoding the body’s molecular language in real time: no guesswork, no gimmicks, just pure #biochemical signal. The round, led by Interlagos with Overwater Ventures, Starbloom Capital, Stanford University, Chan Zuckerberg Biohub Network, Hyperlink Ventures, Cantos, Humba Ventures, and Seaside Equity Partners, pushes total funding to $23M since 2022. That’s not luck, it’s physics, chemistry, and 17 yrs of Stanford & CZ Biohub research finally moving out of the lab and onto your skin. Co-founders Vijit Sabnis (CEO), Alex Yoshikawa (CSO), and Pawan Kapur (CTO) built Adaptyx on molecular intelligence that reads the body’s real-time #chemistry like Spotify reads [...] Let's talk about what happens when science stops chasing youth and starts quantifying it. Generation Lab just raised $11M in seed funding, led by Accel, marking Accel's 1st-ever longevity bet. Not a small move. Accel doesn't throw darts at startup boards. They play chess, and they just made a bold opening move into the biotech future of staying younger, longer. Founded by Alina Rui Su, Dr. Irina Conboy, and Michael Suswal, Generation Lab isn't just talking about #aging; they're measuring it at the molecular level. Alina Rui Su, Harvard Medical School PhD, University of California, Berkeley bioengineering alum, Forbes 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose #research on plasma dilution flipped

# Accel invests in Generation Lab, a longevity startup.

This title was summarized by AI from the post below.

[Delphine Le Grand](https://www.linkedin.com/in/delphinelegrandofficial?trk=public_post_feed-actor-name)

* [Report this post](/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fdelphinelegrandofficial_accels-first-longevity-bet-generation-activity-7389309878919626753-B2CJ&trk=public_post_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)

[Accel](https://www.linkedin.com/company/accel-vc?trk=public_post-text)'s first longevity bet? [Generation Lab](https://www.linkedin.com/company/generation-lab-longevity?trk=public_post-text) and its $11M seed raise. Last week, one of Silicon Valley’s top venture firms made it official, Accel is going all in on longevity with Gen Lab. Their goal? To make measuring how fast we’re aging as routine as "measuring cholesterol". They've already partnered with more than 300 clinics (and as someone who gets asked all the time how to get access to clinics, trust me this is no small feat), offering tools that help physicians quantify biological aging through blood, epigenetic, and phenotypic data. But the real play goes beyond tests, it’s about the data infrastructure for aging itself. → 𝗔 𝗻𝗲𝘄 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗿𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘂𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗮𝗴𝗶𝗻𝗴 Most diagnostic data sits in silos. Gen Lab is building the connective tissue that links biomarker testing, patient data, and predictive models. That unlocks a new category of longitudinal insights, tracking aging like we track disease. → 𝗟𝗼𝗻𝗴𝗲𝘃𝗶𝘁𝘆 𝗴𝗼𝗲𝘀 𝗰𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗶𝗰𝗮𝗹 What started with wearables and wellness labs is now entering medical practice. The next decade of longevity will combine software, data, and physician networks. → 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝗶𝗴𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗔𝗰𝗰𝗲𝗹 When a firm known for backing facebook, spotify, dropbox makes its first longevity investment, it’s more than capital, it’s validation that this field has crossed the threshold from speculation to inevitability (let's gooooooo). TLDR - Generation Lab is bridging consumer curiosity and clinical credibility, and it’s especially inspiring to see women like [Alina Rui Su](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alinasu?trk=public_post-text) leading the charge in shaping what the future of longevity looks like. ♻️ Repost if you’re following the evolution of longevity diagnostics. Follow me at [Delphine Le Grand](https://www.linkedin.com/in/delphinelegrandofficial?trk=public_post-text) for more from the edge of health, science, and innovation.

Love this move—and agree the real moat is the data rail, not the single test. What I’m watching to separate signal from hype: -Standards & interoperability: FHIR-first pipes, harmonized biomarker ontologies, and provenance so multi-omics isn’t apples/oranges. -Clinical proof → reimbursement: prospective outcomes, CPT pathways, and cost-offset models (risk stratification that actually lowers spend). -Model governance: drift monitoring, transparent feature importance, and patient-level explanations clinicians trust. -Reference ranges that move: aging signals are longitudinal—does the platform recalibrate per cohort, sex, life stage, meds? -Data rights & portability: clean consent, revocation, and patient-owned vaults so clinics don’t feel locked-in and regulators don’t balk. If Gen Lab nails clinician workflow + outcomes + payer logic, longevity becomes a category—not a gadget. Excited to see Alina Rui Su push this across the credibility chasm.

We're seeing a convergence of affordable genomics, AI-powered analysis, and consumer willingness to pay for health optimization. Generation Lab sits at that intersection. For medtech and healthtech investors: longevity diagnostics represent a massive TAM with subscription revenue models and improving unit economics as testing costs decline. Alina Rui Su, [Harvard Medical School](https://www.linkedin.com/school/harvard-medical-school/?trk=public_post-text) PhD, [University of California, Berkeley](https://www.linkedin.com/school/uc-berkeley/?trk=public_post-text) bioengineering alum, [Forbes](https://www.linkedin.com/company/forbes-magazine?trk=public_post-text) 30 Under 30, and someone who filed her 1st medical patent at 15, teamed up with Dr. Irina Conboy, the UC Berkeley professor whose [#research](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fresearch&trk=public_post-text) on plasma dilution flipped [#agingbiology](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fagingbiology&trk=public_post-text) on its head. Add Michael Suswal, co-founder of [Standard AI](https://www.linkedin.com/company/standard-ai?trk=public_post-text) and a man who knows how to scale systems from lab bench to market trench, and you've got a team built for impact. Their platform, SystemAge™, doesn't guess your [#biologicalage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbiologicalage&trk=public_post-text), it measures it. With over 460 [#epigenetic](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fepigenetic&trk=public_post-text) markers across 19 organ systems, Generation Lab can tell you how old your [#brain](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrain&trk=public_post-text), [#heart](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fheart&trk=public_post-text), [#liver](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fliver&trk=public_post-text), or [#immunesystem](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fimmunesystem&trk=public_post-text) really is. They call it "measuring biological noise." Translation: the closer your body is to static, the younger you are. Clinical validation already shows results like a 13.6-year [#brainage](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbrainage&trk=public_post-text) reversal using MUSE [#Stemcell](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fstemcell&trk=public_post-text) treatments. That's not sci-fi. That's data. This seed round, featuring [Samsung Next](https://www.linkedin.com/company/samsung-next?trk=public_post-text), [Zone2](https://www.linkedin.com/company/z2-zone2?trk=public_post-text), Steve Aoki's [Aoki Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/aokilabs?trk=public_post-text), Giannis Antetokounmpo's [BYL Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/bylventures?trk=public_post-text), and [Simu Liu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/simu-liu-47946a2b1?trk=public_post-text)'s [Markham Valley Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/markhamvalley?trk=public_post-text), pushes total funding to $15M. Big names, sure, but more importantly, diverse minds from entertainment, sports, and tech betting on [#longevity](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flongevity&trk=public_post-text) as the next frontier. For investors, Envue’s story is a reminder that Deep tech exits don’t behave like SaaS multiples. ✅They mature slowly, compound quietly, and then unlock disproportionate value once industry incumbents catch up. ✅The best returns often come from the least obvious founders. ✅Ecosystems matter. Networks accelerate readiness, confidence, and alignment. Ellen said it best: “You don’t need a perfect plan, just the courage to start. They’re building the future, one molecule at a time.

The round, led by [Interlagos](https://www.linkedin.com/company/interlagoscapital?trk=public_post-text) with [Overwater Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/overwater-ventures?trk=public_post-text), [Starbloom Capital](https://www.linkedin.com/company/starbloom-capital?trk=public_post-text), [Stanford University](https://www.linkedin.com/school/stanford-university/?trk=public_post-text), [Chan Zuckerberg Biohub Network](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cz-biohub?trk=public_post-text), [Hyperlink Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/hyperlinkventures?trk=public_post-text), [Cantos](https://www.linkedin.com/company/cantos-ventures?trk=public_post-text), [Humba Ventures](https://www.linkedin.com/company/humba-ventures?trk=public_post-text), and [Seaside Equity Partners](https://www.linkedin.com/company/seaside-equity-partners?trk=public_post-text), pushes total funding to $23M since 2022. That’s not luck, it’s physics, chemistry, and 17 yrs of Stanford & CZ Biohub research finally moving out of the lab and onto your skin. Co-founders [Vijit Sabnis](https://www.linkedin.com/in/vijitsabnis?trk=public_post-text) (CEO), [Alex Yoshikawa](https://www.linkedin.com/in/alex-yoshikawa?trk=public_post-text) (CSO), and [Pawan Kapur](https://www.linkedin.com/in/pawan-kapur-83723515?trk=public_post-text) (CTO) built Adaptyx on molecular intelligence that reads the body’s real-time [#chemistry](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fchemistry&trk=public_post-text) like Spotify reads playlists, continuously and personally. Sabnis, ex-Khosla Ventures partner and Solar Junction founder, knows how to turn science into industry. Yoshikawa earned his Ph.D. under [H. Tom Soh](https://www.linkedin.com/in/h-tom-soh-752224273?trk=public_post-text) at Stanford, while Kapur’s been the quiet hardware assassin behind Mojo Vision & Sunpreme. Add Professors Soh & [Joseph DeSimone](https://www.linkedin.com/in/josephdesimone1?trk=public_post-text), both Stanford heavyweights, and you’ve got a founding roster that could publish in Nature before breakfast. The tech hits different. Adaptyx’s platform uses programmable [#DNA](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdna&trk=public_post-text) switches that light up when analytes, glucose, potassium, lactate, cortisol, appear in your [#bloodstream](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fbloodstream&trk=public_post-text). It’s a nightclub for molecules, with fluorescence instead of strobe lights. That signal becomes a continuous molecular feed, translating the chaos of biochemistry into a clear story about how your body’s actually doing. Forget snapshots. This is the movie. The implications are massive: continuous [#heartfailure](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fheartfailure&trk=public_post-text) monitoring, real-time [#hormone](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fhormone&trk=public_post-text) & [#metabolism](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fmetabolism&trk=public_post-text) tracking, and precision [#drugdosing](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdrugdosing&trk=public_post-text) that could redefine critical care. The prototype tracks five biomarkers, potassium, sodium, creatinine, urea, NT-proBNP, and next expands to hundreds more. Think of it as a molecular search engine for human health. Leading investors’ side, [Achal Upadhyaya](https://www.linkedin.com/in/achal-upadhyaya?trk=public_post-text) of Interlagos joins the board after a decade at SpaceX, because who better to scale precision from rockets to biowearables? [Kristina Simmons](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kristina-simmons-6964647?trk=public_post-text) of Overwater (ex-Khosla, a16z) brings the operational gravity that keeps startups from drifting. The capital fuels R&D, clinical trials, and growth across [#engineering](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fengineering&trk=public_post-text), [#datascience](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fdatascience&trk=public_post-text) & [#clinicalops](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Fclinicalops&trk=public_post-text), all aimed at [FDA](https://www.linkedin.com/company/fda?trk=public_post-text) clearance before a wellness rollout. Adaptyx isn’t chasing trends; it’s building infrastructure for how we’ll monitor health in the 2030s, a bridge between the quantified and clinically verified self. Continuous monitoring just got molecular.
